title: Fix pulling from secure registry
body:
when pulling from a secure registry that is documented in registries.conf, we
should be able to pull without tls-verify=false

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
:pushpin: Commit 6078812 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 60788124b7017eb6424ec27cbe1f1a9bb1771cd8 -->
@baude Could you explain how this commit is fixing the mentioned issue? It somehow looks to be equivalent, isn't it?

Could you expand on the description a bit @baude?  Are you saying that if the registry is in the list of secured registries in registries.conf, you don't need to pass in '--tls-verify=false' if you're trying to connect to it without un/pwd?  That doesn't sound right.  I'd be hip if you dropped '--tls-verify=true' when trying to connect to it.

I'm not quite sure what's going on under the covers.  But if the registry is on the secure list, I think we should by default be passing `--tls-verify=true' and if the registry is on the insecure list, then we should by default pass '--tls-verify=false'.

@vrothberg It is not equiv.  It fixes a bug.  The means to enter the condition in question was always failing because the transport names differ.  One was `docker` and the other was `docker://`; hence the use of prefix.

:sunny: Test successful - [status-papr](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/b44d4fb1ec05aec958a63bdfa3d344cdf4b4da8f.1525444804544374784/index.html)
Approved by: rhatdan
Pushing b44d4fb1ec05aec958a63bdfa3d344cdf4b4da8f to master...
